perturbation.original_text: All materials and Confidential Infor mation shall remain the exclusive property of the Disclosing Party. Except as expressly provided herein or under a separate written agreement between the parties that references this Agreement, either party shall not be obligated to grant, convey or transfer to the other any interest, license or other right, or under its Confidential Information or any patent, copyright, trade secret, trademark or other intellectual property right.
perturbation.changed_text: All materials and Confidential Infor mation shall remain the exclusive property of the Receiving Party. Except as expressly provided herein or under a separate written agreement between the parties that references this Agreement, either party shall not be obligated to grant, convey or transfer to the other any interest, license or other right, or under its Confidential Information or any patent, copyright, trade secret, trademark or other intellectual property right.
perturbation.explanation: The change from 'Disclosing Party' to 'Receiving Party' creates a direct contradiction regarding ownership of confidential information. The contract now states that the Receiving Party owns the confidential information, which undermines the entire purpose of the NDA, which is to protect the Disclosing Party's confidential information. This affects all sections dealing with the use, protection, and return of confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Upon the earlier of (i) the completion or (ii) the Disclosing Party’s written r equest, the Receiving Party shall promptly return or destroy all Confidential Information disclosed or made available by the Disclosing Party, in any form and including, all Notes, save to the extent that regulation, applicable rule, law or record retention policy requires retention of such material. Upon request, the Receiving Party shall certify in writing that it has returned or destroyed all Confidential Information in accordance with this paragraph 6, disclosed or made available by the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The original text states the Disclosing Party retains exclusive property of confidential information, aligning with the contradicted text which details the Receiving Party returning information disclosed by the Disclosing Party. The changed text, however, assigns ownership to the Receiving Party. This directly contradicts the section which requires the Receiving Party to return or destroy confidential information 'disclosed or made available by the Disclosing Party', as the Receiving Party cannot logically return property it now owns according to the modified clause. This creates a strong, in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Upon the earlier of (i) the completion or (ii) the Disclosing Party’s written r equest, the Receiving Party shall promptly return or destroy all Confidential Information disclosed or made available by the Disclosing Party, in any form and including, all Notes, save to the extent that regulation, applicable rule, law or record retention policy requires retention of such material. Upon request, the Receiving Party shall certify in writing that it has returned or destroyed all Confidential Information in accordance with this paragraph 6, disclosed or made available by the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon the earlier of (i) the completion or (ii) the Disclosing Party’s written r equest, the Receiving Party shall retain all Confidential Information disclosed or made available by the Disclosing Party, in any form and including, all Notes, save to the extent that regulation, applicable rule, law or record retention policy requires retention of such material. Upon request, the Receiving Party shall certify in writing that it has returned or destroyed all Confidential Information in accordance with this paragraph 6, disclosed or made available by the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'return or destroy' to 'retain,' the clause now contradicts itself. The initial statement requires retention of information, while the subsequent sentence still requires the Receiving Party to certify that they have 'returned or destroyed' the information, even though they are now obligated to retain it. This creates an impossible situation and significant ambiguity about the Receiving Party's obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Upon the earlier of (i) the completion or (ii) the Disclosing Party’s written r equest, the Receiving Party shall retain all Confidential Information disclosed or made available by the Disclosing Party, in any form and including, all Notes, save to the extent that regulation, applicable rule, law or record retention policy requires retention of such material. Upon request, the Receiving Party shall certify in writing that it has returned or destroyed all Confidential Information in accordance with this paragraph 6, disclosed or made available by the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the original text from 'return or destroy' to 'retain'. The contradicted text, which is the same as the changed text, now requires the Receiving Party to 'retain' confidential information. However, it still includes the clause that the Receiving Party must certify in writing that it has 'returned or destroyed' the information, which directly conflicts with the requirement to retain it. This creates a clear and strong in-text contradiction as per the explanation provided. The definition 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' is triggered.
